content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 to NNN) rooted at node 111.

Node iii has value Ai(1‚Č§Au‚Č§109)A_i(1 \leq A_u \leq 10^9)Ai‚Äč(1‚Č§Au‚Äč‚Č§109) associated to it.

You will receive QQQ queries, each containing two nodes uuu and vvv. For each query:


First swap values AuA_uAu‚Äč and AvA_vAv‚Äč ;
Suppose PxP_xPx‚Äč denotes the array containing the values of all nodes in the subtree of xxx (including node xxx) in non-decreasing order.
Suppose array LLL is the lexicographically smallest array among P1,P2,‚Ä¶PNP_1, P_2, \ldots P_NP1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶PN‚Äč.
Find the sum of all u(1‚Č§u‚Č§N)u(1 \leq u \leq N)u(1‚Č§u‚Č§N) such that Pu=LP_u = LPu‚Äč=L.
Note that the swaps are persistent between queries. In other words, the answer for a query is dependent on the swaps made during earlier queries.

Note that an array SSS is said to be lexicographically smaller than array TTT, if, either:


SSS is a prefix of TTT and ‚ą£S‚ą£<‚ą£T‚ą£|S| \lt |T|‚ą£S‚ą£<‚ą£T‚ą£, or;
In the first position where SSS and TTT differ, array SSS has an element that is smaller than the corresponding element in array TTT.
For example, [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] is lexicographically smaller than [1,2,4],[1,2,3,4],[1, 2, 4], [1, 2, 3, 4],[1,2,4],[1,2,3,4], and [2][2][2].


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains two positive integers NNN and QQQ, the number of nodes and the number of queries respectively.
The second line contains NNN space separated integers, A1,A2‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2 \ldots A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč‚Ä¶AN‚Äč ‚ąí-‚ąí denoting the array AAA.
The following next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contains two integers uuu and vvv, denoting an edge between nodes uuu and vvv. It is guaranteed that the given edges form a tree.
The following next QQQ lines contains two integers uuu and vvv, the nodes whose value should be swapped.


Output Format
For each query, print the sum of all u(1‚Č§u‚Č§N)u(1 \leq u \leq N)u(1‚Č§u‚Č§N) such that Pu=LP_u = LPu‚Äč=L.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
